net_income: Net loss of $4.7 million, an improvement from a net loss of $32.8 million YoY
key_metrics: Food and beverage revenue: $499.6 million (up 36.1% YoY)
Operating income: $92.6 million (improved from an operating loss of $47.4 million YoY)
qoq_changes: Strong sequential revenue growth, with Q2 revenue of $1,397.9 million significantly outpacing implied Q1 2025 revenue of $862.5 million.
Cash and cash equivalents decreased to $423.7 million from $632.3 million at the end of Q4 2024.
new_risks: Interest expense on corporate borrowings increased 22.9% YoY to $109.6 million, reflecting the growing burden of debt servicing.
Liquidity contraction, with cash reserves dropping by over $200 million in the first six months of 2025 despite improved operating income.
management_tone: Cautiously optimistic, as strong box office performance drove a 35.6% revenue increase and a return to positive operating income, though heavy debt and interest expenses continue to prevent net profitability.
analyst_note: AMC demonstrated robust top-line recovery in Q2 2025, driven by strong admissions and concession sales that pushed operating income into the black. However, the company's highly leveraged balance sheet ($3.99 billion in corporate borrowings) and shrinking cash reserves remain significant structural overhangs.
